questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: MINER : ORE :: FARMER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'worker : product extracted/produced'.

**Working.** A miner extracts ore; a farmer produces a crop. A tractor and field are tools/locations, not products.

**Answer.** crop.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Arrange the sentences into a coherent paragraph.
P: Finally, the processed ore is transported to the smelter.
Q: First, the ore body is located through exploratory drilling.
R: Then the ore is extracted and hauled to the surface.
S: Next, it is crushed and concentrated at the mill.
The correct order is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order the steps by their explicit sequence markers (First, Then, Next, Finally).

**Working.** First locate (Q), then extract and haul (R), next crush/concentrate (S), finally transport to smelter (P).

**Answer.** Q, R, S, P.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A circle is inscribed in a square of side $10$ (figure). Compute the shaded area outside the circle but inside the square (use $\pi=3.1416$, two decimals).

figure.caption: Inscribed circle in a square
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='25' y='25' width='120' height='120' fill='#2563eb'/><circle cx='85' cy='85' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb'/><text x='30' y='168' font-size='8'>square 10, inscribed circle</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shaded $=$ square area $-$ circle area, with $r=5$.

**Working.**
$$A=10^2-3.1416(5)^2=100-78.54=21.46$$

**Answer.** $21.46$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The measure of each interior angle of a regular hexagon is _____ degrees.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a class of $40$, $22$ play cricket, $18$ play football and $6$ play both. The number who play only cricket is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: X is the brother of the son of Y's son. How is X related to Y?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve generations from the inside out.

**Working.** 'The son of Y's son' is Y's grandson; X is his brother, hence also Y's grandson.

**Answer.** Grandson.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: On an equal-area (Schmidt) stereonet, a plane is represented as a

figure.caption: A dipping plane as a great circle

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A plane plots as a great circle; its pole plots as a point.

**Working.**
Great circle.

**Answer.** Great circle

questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: A low initial $^{87}$Sr/$^{86}$Sr ratio (~0.703) in a basalt indicates
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Low initial Sr ratios reflect a depleted (low Rb/Sr) mantle source.

**Working.**
Mantle source.

**Answer.** Depleted mantle

questions.subject: Metamorphic Petrology
questions.stem: The metamorphic facies characterised by high pressure and low temperature is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Blueschist = high-P/low-T (subduction); granulite = high-T; hornfels = contact.

**Working.**
Blueschist.

**Answer.** Blueschist

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: A rock joint has cohesion $50\,$kPa and friction angle $25^\circ$. Under normal stress $50\,$kPa, the shear strength is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$

questions.solution:

**Working.**

**Answer.** Straight-line segments
